title: InvisiClip™ held 306 psf. What wind-uplift testing means for your deck.
excerpt: American Pro grooved decking on InvisiClip™ was tested by Intertek under ICC-ES AC174 using ASTM E330. Three specimens averaged 306 psf ultimate uplift and 292 psf sustained. What that number means for a hidden-fastener deck in a high-wind or coastal setting, and why we tested our own boards instead of borrowing a spec sheet.
heroAlt: A grooved PVC deck board hovering above two InvisiClip hidden fasteners on an aluminum rail, with the board grooves aligned to the clip cradles
author: American Pro

title: A lakeside deck in Aged Oak. Nothing on the surface but the view.
excerpt: A tired wood deck on a New England lake gets rebuilt in TrueGrain Deck Aged Oak with InvisiClip hidden fasteners. No screws on the surface, no splinters underfoot, and a floor that stays cool at golden hour. The project, the choices, and the finished result.
heroAlt: A new American Pro TrueGrain Deck floor in Aged Oak on a New England lake home at golden hour, with a clean fastener-free surface, black cable railing, and two Adirondack chairs facing the water
author: American Pro

title: Why a PVC deck stays cooler in July.
excerpt: Dark decks can get too hot for bare feet by noon. Here is the building science behind deck surface temperature, plus how TrueGrain Deck's ASA cap and cellular PVC core are engineered to run measurably cooler under direct summer sun.
heroAlt: Bare feet standing comfortably on a sunlit American Pro TrueGrain walnut PVC deck beside a backyard pool in full midday summer sun
author: American Pro

title: PVC mouldings don't rot. What that actually means for your trim.
excerpt: Wood trim absorbs water and rots from the inside. American Pro PVC mouldings, brick mould, sills, casings, and crowns are cellular PVC, so they will not rot, split, or wick moisture. What to spec, how to install, and why it changes how a house ages.
heroAlt: American Pro PVC architectural mouldings stacked at the Patwin Plastics extrusion facility in Linden, New Jersey, showing crowns, casings, sills, and brick mould profiles in bright white finish
author: American Pro

title: Front porch reborn. A 1908 Victorian gets a tongue-and-groove PVC porch in Tuscany.
excerpt: A turn-of-the-century Queen Anne loses its rotted wood porch and gets a new tongue-and-groove cellular PVC floor in Tuscany. The decision, the install, and what it looks like in golden hour.
heroAlt: Restored 1908 Queen Anne Victorian front porch with new American Pro Tuscany cellular PVC tongue-and-groove flooring, white turned-spindle railing, hanging fern, and hydrangeas at golden hour
author: American Pro

title: Inside TrueGrain Deck. How the board is built.
excerpt: Five layers of RENOLIT PMMA film over a Patwin proprietary ASA capstock over a cellular PVC core with 30% in-house regrind. The chemistry, the construction, and what it means on a finished deck.
heroAlt: TrueGrain Deck Tropical Walnut PVC decking installed on a coastal home at late-afternoon light
author: American Pro

title: Spring deck planning checklist for a summer 2026 install.
excerpt: Mid-May is the week a summer deck gets specced. Footprint, board, fasteners, joist spacing, samples, and the one-page job spec we hand every contractor before the first board ships.
heroAlt: Walnut PVC deck installed on a craftsman home at golden hour, with patio furniture and a clean railing line
author: American Pro

title: Introducing InvisiClip™. Hidden hardware. Honest deck.
excerpt: POM clips on aluminum rails. Boards drop in from above. No screws on the deck surface. Powered by the GRAD® system.
heroAlt: Walnut PVC deck board hovering above two InvisiClip hidden fasteners on an aluminum rail, grooves aligned with the clips
author: American Pro

title: Where to buy American Pro on the East Coast.
excerpt: Two trusted regional partners are stocking American Pro right now. Genesee Reserve Supply for porch flooring across Western NY and the Finger Lakes, and Stonewood Products for PVC decking on Cape Cod and SE Massachusetts. Phone numbers, hours, and territory.
heroAlt: Walnut and grey PVC decking bundles being loaded onto a flatbed at an East Coast wholesale building products dealer yard at golden hour
author: American Pro

title: Made in Linden, New Jersey. Where American Pro decking is extruded.
excerpt: We mix our own PVC compound, cut our own dies, and run our own tooling for American Pro decking, porch, and mouldings. Here is what “made in Linden” actually means, and where the line is between what we extrude and what is GRAD®-licensed.
heroAlt: A walnut wood-grain PVC deck board exiting an extrusion die head and traveling along stainless cooling rollers inside an American Pro production line in Linden, New Jersey
author: American Pro
